operating_state: Total revenue reached $164.5 billion in 2024, representing 22% growth driven by advertising revenue expansion across Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp platforms. Family Daily Active People grew 5% to 3.35 billion with worldwide average revenue per person increasing 15% to $49.63, reflecting strong user engagement and monetization efficiency. Ad impressions delivered increased 11% while average price per ad rose 10%, demonstrating pricing power despite regulatory headwinds. Reality Labs revenue grew 13% from consumer hardware sales though the segment recorded substantial operating losses.
cost_structure: Operating expenses increased to $95.1 billion with research and development costs driven by AI infrastructure investments and Reality Labs development. Cost of revenue rose 16% primarily from data center depreciation expense, reflecting substantial infrastructure expansion. Share-based compensation totaled $16.7 billion, up from $14.0 billion, while the company extended server and network asset useful lives to 5.5 years effective 2025, reducing future depreciation by approximately $2.9 billion annually.
material_events: Meta initiated its first quarterly dividend program at $0.50 per share, paying $5.1 billion in total dividends during 2024. The company repurchased $29.8 billion of Class A shares and issued $10.4 billion in new fixed-rate senior unsecured notes. European Commission issued EUR 798 million competition fine for Facebook Marketplace practices, currently under appeal. Board expanded to thirteen members with appointment of three new independent directors in December 2024.
macro_exposures: European regulatory developments created significant compliance costs and operational constraints, with Digital Markets Act requiring subscription model modifications potentially impacting Q3 2025 revenue. Foreign currency transaction losses increased to $690 million from $366 million, reflecting heightened international exposure. IRS transfer pricing disputes involve potential additional federal tax liability up to $9.0 billion for 2010-2013 tax years, with Tax Court decision expected in 2025.
forward_outlook: Management projects 2025 capital expenditures of $64-72 billion to support AI infrastructure and generative capabilities, with Reality Labs operating losses expected to increase significantly. Effective tax rate anticipated at 12-15% for 2025 assuming current stock price levels. European business modifications required by Q3 2025 following DMA decision could materially impact user experience and revenue, though the company plans to appeal the ruling.
uncertainty_profile: FTC administrative proceeding seeks substantial consent order modifications including prohibition on minors' data usage and board composition changes, with potential severe operational restrictions. Multiple youth-related litigation proceedings alleging social media addiction face first personal injury trial in November 2025. EU-US Data Privacy Framework faces potential invalidation that could prevent Facebook and Instagram operations in Europe with material adverse business impact.
